title: Golden Irish Potato Galette
description: A nutty, creamy, and nostalgic Irish dish featuring a lamination-puff pastry crust, perfect for a comforting snack or dinner.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 3mm and transfer it to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
In a bowl, mix the grated cheese, chopped rosemary, minced garlic, and red chilli flakes.
Thinly slice the Irish potatoes and layer them on top of the pastry, leaving a 5cm border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the pastry up over the potatoes to create a crust, pressing gently to seal.
Dot the top of the potatoes with butter and sprinkle with the cheese mixture.
Brush the edges of the pastry with a little water and fold the remaining pastry over the potatoes to create a galette shape.
Bake the galette in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
Remove the galette from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
